description: Replace plain Error throw in get-current-user.ts with ToolError.internal, and remove unreachable response.ok checks after fetchFromPage in tripadvisor-api.ts.
qualityChecks: cd plugins/tripadvisor && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Replace throw new Error() in get-current-user.ts with ToolError.internal()
userStories.description: In plugins/tripadvisor/src/tools/get-current-user.ts line 19, `throw new Error('Could not find member profile in SSR data.')` bypasses the ToolError dispatch chain. Replace with ToolError.internal().
acceptanceCriteria: Line 19 in get-current-user.ts uses ToolError.internal('Could not find member profile in SSR data.') instead of new Error()
ToolError is imported from '@opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk' in the file
userStories.notes:
Files:
- plugins/tripadvisor/src/tools/get-current-user.ts (line 19)

Context:
Line 19:
  if (!memberProfile) throw new Error('Could not find member profile in SSR data.');

Change to:
  if (!memberProfile) throw ToolError.internal('Could not find member profile in SSR data.');

Make sure ToolError is imported from '@opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk'. It likely needs to be added to the import.

userStories.title: Remove dead response.ok checks after fetchFromPage in tripadvisor-api.ts
userStories.description: In plugins/tripadvisor/src/tripadvisor-api.ts, the graphql() function calls fetchFromPage then has two unreachable checks: `if (response.status === 401 || response.status === 403)` and `if (!response.ok)` at lines 68-76. Since fetchFromPage auto-throws ToolError on non-ok responses (including 401/403), these checks are dead code. Remove them.
acceptanceCriteria: The `if (response.status === 401 || response.status === 403)` block is removed from tripadvisor-api.ts
The `if (!response.ok)` block is removed from tripadvisor-api.ts
The JSON parsing and data extraction that follow remain unchanged
